new_classes.label_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength by volume of 80% vol or higher (CN 2207 10 00)
new_classes.label_de: Ethylalkohol mit einem Alkoholgehalt von 80 % vol oder mehr, unvergällt (KN 2207 10 00)
new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength by volume of 80% vol or higher, as described in CN subheading 2207 10 00. Excludes spirituous beverages (e.g. gin, vodka), which fall under subheadings 2208 20 12 to 2208 90 78 regardless of their alcoholic strength.
Quality borne by an ethyl alcohol product indicating that it has not been denatured (i.e. no substances have been added to render it unfit for human consumption).

new_classes.label_de: Alkoholgehalt von 80 % vol oder mehr – Eigenschaft
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by an ethyl alcohol product indicating that its actual alcoholic strength by volume is 80% vol or higher.
Quality indicating that the product is not a spirituous beverage (such as gin or vodka) that would fall under CN subheadings 2208 20 12 to 2208 90 78.

coverage_explanation: The three principal discriminating criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) the product is ethyl alcohol (material entity subclass of Beverage/Chapter 22 ethyl alcohol category); (2) it is undenatured (UndenaturedEthylAlcoholQuality); (3) its alcoholic strength is ≥ 80% vol (AlcoholicStrength80PlusVolQuality with decimalRange restriction). The exclusion of spirituous beverages (gin, vodka) classified under 2208 20 12 – 2208 90 78 is modelled via a complement restriction referencing SpiritsLiqueursOtherSpiritousBeverages2208. A minor gap exists: the HS Explanatory Note reference (excluding its fourth paragraph) contains further technical guidance about what counts as undenatured ethyl alcohol that cannot be fully formalised in OWL without additional quality/role classes not yet present in the TBox. The complement restriction is an approximation — OWL DL does not allow direct range-based code exclusions — so the exclusion of 2208 spirituous beverages is expressed as a complement on the sibling 2208 class rather than an enumeration of individual sub-codes 2208 20 12 to 2208 90 78.
